title: oenothera primiveris subsp. bufonis
intro: Oenothera primiveris subsp. bufonis, also known as the Toad's Morning Glory, is a subspecies of the evening primrose plant that is native to certain regions of North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Oenothera primiveris subsp. bufonis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and salves that can be applied topically to the skin or ingested to promote overall well-being.
